title: Rich and Spicy Malaysian Chicken Curry
description: A flavorful Malaysian curry made with mustardy spices, crunchy peanuts, and a hint of passion fruit. This dish is perfect for meal prep and is halal-friendly.
instructions: Marinate the chicken in a mixture of miso paste, coconut milk, and spices for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a hot pot vessel over medium-high heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and saut8 until the onion is translucent.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the peanuts, mustard seeds, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder and cook for 1 minute.
Add the passion fruit pulp, fish sauce, lime juice, salt, and black pepper and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 10-15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, crushed peanuts, sliced scallions, and sliced lime wedges.
Serve hot over rice or with roti.
Reheat in the hot pot vessel over low heat for up to 30 minutes.
Adjust the seasoning as needed and serve hot.
Serve with steamed vegetables or naan bread for a complete meal.
Serve as a snack or appetizer with crackers or crostini.
Serve as a side dish or as a main course with a side of steamed vegetables.
Serve as a topping for a salad or as a garnish for a soup.
Serve as a filling for a sandwich or as a topping for a wrap.
Serve as a side dish or as a main course with a side of steamed vegetables and a side of rice.
Serve as a snack or appetizer with crackers or crostini and a side of hummus.
